text:
ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE — POST-CYCLE 1 IPILIMUMAB/NIVOLUMAB

ATTENDING: [Oncologist]
DEPARTMENT: Medical Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Return visit for toxicity assessment and cycle 2 planning following first infusion of ipilimumab (Yervoy) 3 mg/kg + nivolumab (Opdivo) 1 mg/kg.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Castellano is a 55-year-old woman with stage IV BRAF V600E-mutant nodular melanoma who presents today for her post-cycle 1 check-in and to plan cycle 2 of combination immunotherapy. As a reminder, she was initiated on ipi/nivo (Yervoy 3 mg/kg / Opdivo 1 mg/kg q3w) following disease progression on dabrafenib/trametinib (dab/tram) at approximately 8 months, which at that time included development of CNS metastases. She underwent SRS to 2 brain lesions prior to starting immunotherapy, which was completed without acute complications. Today is approximately 3 weeks following her first cycle.

Since her last visit she has been at home and is functioning reasonably well. She reports mild fatigue, which began roughly 10 days post-infusion and she describes as a 3–4/10 in severity. She is still working part-time as a landscape architect and managing her household without assistance, though she notes she is napping in the afternoon more than her baseline. She is eating well with no significant change in appetite.

She reports the development of a pruritic rash on her upper trunk and back, which started about 2 weeks after her infusion. She describes it as diffuse, slightly raised, and pink — somewhat resembling mild eczema. There is no blistering, no mucosal involvement, and no involvement of her face or extremities. She has not had fevers, lip swelling, or throat tightness. She tried over-the-counter hydrocortisone cream initially with minimal effect; she called our office triage line and was prescribed triamcinolone 0.1% cream, which she reports is providing moderate relief. Overall she rates the rash as manageable and says she is not distressed by it.

She explicitly denies diarrhea, hematochezia, urgency, or increased stool frequency. No abdominal cramping. No nausea or vomiting. No jaundice or scleral icterus. She reports no joint pain, no muscle weakness, no vision changes, no headaches, and no new neurologic symptoms. She specifically denies any symptoms referable to the prior brain lesion sites. Denies dysuria, polyuria, or polydipsia. No new shortness of breath or cough.

Prior relevant history: progressed through approximately 8 months of first-line dab/tram. Brain MRI at that time showed 2 metastases; SRS was delivered prior to this immunotherapy regimen. She had a prior episode of drug-related pyrexia on dab/tram, which resolved with dose interruption. BRAF V600E+, TMB 18.4 mut/Mb per NGS.

Current medications include triamcinolone 0.1% cream topically to rash, and prior to this visit was taking levothyroxine (she denies this — TSH was checked; see labs below). No other systemic medications. No corticosteroids.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive: fatigue (mild, grade 1), pruritic rash upper trunk (grade 1).
Negative: no fever/chills, no diarrhea/colitis symptoms, no nausea/vomiting, no dysphagia, no abdominal pain, no jaundice, no joint pain, no myalgias, no headache, no new neurologic deficits, no visual changes, no palpitations, no cough, no dyspnea, no polyuria, no polydipsia, no skin breakdown or blistering.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vitals: BP 118/74, HR 72, RR 16, Temp 36.8°C, SpO2 97% on room air, Wt 68 kg (stable from prior).
ECOG Performance Status: 1.

General: Alert, well-appearing woman in no acute distress. Conversational and engaged.
Skin: Diffuse, maculopapular, erythematous rash over upper trunk, chest, and mid-back. Non-confluent. No vesicles, bullae, or desquamation. No mucosal involvement. Rash estimated to cover approximately 10–15% BSA. Grade 1 per CTCAE. No excoriations or secondary infection noted. Remainder of skin: no new subcutaneous nodules palpated (prior subcutaneous metastases not palpable today; consistent with prior imaging response on dab/tram and subsequent interval).
HEENT: Anicteric sclerae. Moist mucous membranes. Oropharynx clear. No oral ulcers.
Neck: No palpable cervical or supraclavicular adenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes or crackles.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Bowel sounds normal. No hepatomegaly. No splenomegaly. No voluntary guarding.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. No synovitis.
Neurological: Alert and oriented x3. Cranial nerves grossly intact. No focal deficits. Gait steady.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
CBC: WBC 7.2 (diff: neutrophils 68%, lymphocytes 22%), Hgb 11.9, Plt 198. Mild normocytic anemia, stable from prior.
CMP: Na 139, K 4.1, Cl 101, CO2 26, BUN 14, Cr 0.82, Glucose 94. LFTs: AST 28, ALT 31, Alk Phos 72, T. bili 0.6 — all within normal limits.
TSH: 2.1 mIU/L — within normal range (no hypothyroidism at this time).
Cortisol (AM): not checked this visit (will reassess if clinical concern arises).
Lipase: not checked (no abdominal symptoms).

---

IMAGING
No new imaging today. Most recent restaging scan from approximately one month prior (prior to starting ipi/nivo) showed interval hepatic lesion progression and new brain metastases, which prompted switch to current regimen. Will plan restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and brain MRI at approximately 12 weeks (3 months) on regimen per standard practice.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Stage IV BRAF V600E+ melanoma, on cycle 1 of Yervoy/Opdivo (ipi 3 mg/kg + nivo 1 mg/kg q3w).
   - Post-cycle 1 assessment today. No major toxicities. Mild expected irAEs as detailed below.
   - Plan to proceed with cycle 2 as scheduled. No dose modifications at this time.
   - Restaging imaging (CT C/A/P + brain MRI) planned around ~12 weeks from start of treatment.

2. irAE — Skin rash, grade 1 (maculopapular, upper trunk, ~10–15% BSA).
   - Consistent with immune-related dermatitis, very common with checkpoint inhibitor therapy, particularly combination ipi + nivo.
   - Currently managed with topical triamcinolone 0.1% cream — patient reports moderate benefit. Will continue.
   - Recommend also adding oral antihistamine PRN (cetirizine 10 mg QD) for pruritus relief.
   - No indication for systemic steroids at this time given grade 1 severity and absence of blistering or mucosal involvement.
   - Patient counseled to monitor for worsening, spread, or development of blisters/skin breakdown — if grade 2+ or mucosal involvement, she should contact us before her next scheduled visit.
   - No dose hold warranted at grade 1.

3. irAE — Fatigue, grade 1.
   - Mild, likely related to immunotherapy. Reviewed sleep hygiene and pacing strategies. No evidence of hypothyroidism (TSH normal today), anemia is mild and stable. No adrenal insufficiency suspected at this point.
   - Will continue to monitor. TSH and AM cortisol to be rechecked at next visit if symptoms persist or worsen.

4. irAE monitoring — Colitis/GI surveillance.
   - Patient is currently asymptomatic from a GI standpoint. Explicitly counseled about the signs/symptoms of immune-mediated colitis: persistent diarrhea (>4 stools/day above baseline), hematochezia, abdominal cramping, urgency. Instructed to call immediately if these develop — will not wait for a scheduled appointment.
   - LFTs normal today — no hepatitis.

5. CNS metastases — S/P SRS.
   - No new neurologic symptoms. Brain MRI will be included in restaging imaging at ~12 weeks. Neurosurgery/radiation oncology to be cc'd on results.

6. Thyroid function — normal today.
   - No levothyroxine indicated currently. Will recheck TSH at next scheduled visit (~3 weeks, pre-cycle 3) and at restaging.

General patient counseling: Ms. Castellano was counseled at length today regarding the nature of immune-related adverse events with combination checkpoint inhibitor therapy — that these can arise at any time during treatment or even weeks after completion of infusions, and that early reporting is critical. She verbalized understanding and was provided with the after-hours oncology triage line. She remains motivated to continue treatment and is tolerating this regimen better than expected subjectively. Her caregiver/partner was not present today but has been previously engaged in her care.

Return to clinic: in approximately 3 weeks for cycle 2 infusion (Yervoy/Opdivo per protocol). Labs to be drawn day of infusion.

Time spent: approximately 35 minutes, more than 50% counseling and care coordination.

— [Attending Oncologist, MD]
[Fellow/PA co-signature if applicable]
